communications.subject: New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: PD Settlement Agreements
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Hampshire Right-to-Know Law, I hereby request the following records:

 A copy of every agreement to settle a lawsuit, threatened lawsuit, or other claim against this agency over the past 10 years. This covers all agreements that would fall under New Hampshire RSA 91-A:4, VI:

VI. Every agreement to settle a lawsuit against a governmental unit, threatened lawsuit, or other claim, entered into by any political subdivision or its insurer, shall be kept on file at the municipal clerk's office and made available for public inspection for a period of no less than 10 years from the date of settlement.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

In some cases, these records may be considered exempt from disclosure because of a privacy interest or some other concern. When considering whether a government record should be released, the courts routinely conduct a balancing test to weigh the public's interest in the records versus the government's interest in non-disclosure. We believe the public interest in these specific records is very high.

Nothing prevents you from making these records available to the public. Both the spirit and letter of the state's Right-to-Know law ensure "the greatest possible public access" to government records and proceedings. We ask that you consider this request carefully and conduct your own balancing test if you are inclined to deny any portion of this request.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jonathan Van Fleet
Concord Monitor
603.369.3303

title: Glendale CO Flock Audits and Records

communications.subject: Colorado Open Records Act Request: Glendale CO Flock Audits and Records
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Colorado Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. Organization Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2022, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency.

2. Network Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2022, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System.

Please provide the audit data in a structured format such as .csv, .xls, or another machine-readable database filetype.

3. Data Sharing
a. The names of agencies and organizations with which the Agency shares ALPR data.
b. The names of agencies and organizations from which the Agency receives ALPR data.
c. The names of agencies and organizations with which the Agency shares “hot list” information.
d. The names of agencies and organizations from which the Agency receives “hot list” information.

4. ALPR Locations: Please provide the precise location of each Flock Safety ALPR camera deployed and utilized by the Glendale Police Department or any related governmental entities.

5. Contracts and Legal Agreements
Copies of all active and recently expired contracts, amendments, renewals, data-sharing agreements, memoranda of understanding, and scope-of-work documents between this agency and Flock Safety. If this agency does not directly work with Flock Safety, please inform me which agency the cameras within Glendale are operated by if possible.

6. Communications with Flock Safety
All emails, letters, or other written communications between agency personnel and any @flocksafety.com email address from January 1, 2022, through the date this request is processed.

You may find that these requests result in a large number of files. These exports from the Flock system are standardized data formats that are inconsequential to redact. To remove private information, simply omit the "License Plate" column. Others have found that other agencies have had trouble with this until they realized they could do this.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Zephyrine Groves

communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: Subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: Student High School Offer Data, SY25-26
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act, I am requesting anonymized aggregate data regarding high school offers for 8th grade students attending elementary schools located in the Back of the Yards/New City community area of Chicago for the 2025-2026 school year. Please make data as updated as possible. Today is May 11th, 2026. 

Specifically, I am requesting data for each elementary school  in Back of the Yards/New City that includes:

The total number of 8th grade students enrolled
The total number of students offered admission to Selective Enrollment high schools
The percentage of 8th grade students offered admission to Selective Enrollment high schools

If available, I also request similar aggregate totals and percentages for other high school offer categories, including but not limited to:

Charter schools
Magnet schools
Military academies
IB programs
CTE programs
Neighborhood schools

To protect student privacy, I am only requesting anonymized aggregate numerical data and not any personally identifiable student information.

If possible, I request that the data be provided in a spreadsheet format such as Excel or CSV.

• Initial incident reports, supplemental reports, and any internal WCSO documentation created in response to the crash
• Internal communications (email, text, Teams, memos) referencing the crash
• Training records for the involved deputy related to emergency driving, pursuit policy, and vehicle operations

2. Body‑Worn Camera & Dash‑Camera Records
Please provide
• Dash‑camera footage from the involved patrol vehicle

3. Communications With Outside Agencies
• All communications between WCSO and Washoe County Sheriff’s Office Risk Management regarding the crash 
• All communications with the Washoe County District Attorney’s Office regarding the crash. 

4. Public‑Facing Materials
 Press releases, drafts, talking points, or prepared statements regarding the crash

title: Modified Request: EEOC Correspondence with Opexus

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Modified Request: EEOC Correspondence with Opexus
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

This is narrowed request relative to my first request about the same matter. Narrowed Request: For dates January 1, 2022 to February 28, 2026, I request all correspondence between EEOC and Opexus management and staff - including staff and management from AINS, which is the former company name of Opexus. 

Background: 
On March 3, 2026, I responded to EEOC's February 26, 2026 acknowledgment notice (or as stated by EEOC to be on February 27, 2026) and stated: "Regarding # 820-2026-005851, I thank you for the response. I do not have an account with EEOC's FOIA Office. As a standing request, ask that EEOC not require me to log into a potentially compromised FOIA system to view any updates , etc. This is especially salient because of recent serious security concerns.... Thank you for considering my requests and desire for secure and transparent communications." Again I do not  have an account with EEOC's FOIA portal and I elect not to use it for reasons I have explained. I request that all communications  occur outside of the EEOC FOIA platform and be transparently sent for public view.

On April 9, 2026, EEOC Office of Legal Counsel sent a letter stating among other things, "This letter is to inform you that we would like to have a conversation with you in hopes of clarifying and narrowing your request. We conducted a search on February 27, 2026, based on your request as written, which resulted in over 124,000 items. We do not have an exact page count, but assuming that doing an industry standard of about three (3)pages per item, the actual page count is going to be likely around the neighborhood of over 372,000 pages. We are putting this request on hold so that we may have a conversation with you concerning how you can effectively limit the scope of this request, so that it can be processed. " Unfortunately EEOC only provided me with 10 days to respond. I was on annual leave during these 10 days. Thus, I am resubmitting this modified request.  

Other Requests and Notices:
As a standing request please provide at least 20 business days for me to respond to any correspondence. In the future, please do not close any of my requests if I do not respond about narrowing a request and continue to process the request until I am able to respond. I have a reasonable accommodations request and ask that communication occur only in writing. This is a standing request. A phone call is not a good option for me. Also, I will always remain interested in receiving all requests until they are received.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jessica Elm

communications.subject: NASA FOIA Request 26-00812-F-HQ
communications.communication:
Good morning, Rich Jones,

This email acknowledges your Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request to the National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA), dated and received in this office on May 11, 2026. Your request was assigned the above-referenced tracking number. You seek the following:

Any and all documents pertaining to summer 2003 vacuum chamber tests in cooperation with Gravitec Inc., conducted at NASA's NSSTC LEEIF facility in Huntsville Alabama.

Under 14 C.F.R. § 1206.301(b), requesters must describe the records sought in sufficient detail to enable Agency personnel who are familiar with the subject area of the request to identify and locate the record with a reasonable amount of effort. Your request does not meet this standard because it uses expansive terms such as "any and all records," which creates an overly broad scope. Requests that are so broad and sweeping that they lack specificity are not reasonably described.

Before NASA can begin processing your request, we need additional information from you. In general, requesters should include as much detail as possible about the records sought. Specifically, please what records you seek. You may also provide public hyperlinks that may assist the FOIA office in conducting a records search (such as news articles referencing an event about which you seek records). If you do not have details about specific records, provide enough event-related information, such as the date and circumstance surrounding the event the record covers, to facilitate the conduct of an organized, non-random search for the requested records. Whenever possible, a request should be specific and include all pertinent details about the requested records, including but not limited to date, title or name, author, recipient, subject matter of the record, case number, file designation, or reference number. It is important to be as specific as possible about what documents should be searched and in what locations (e.g., particular NASA Centers, program offices, and/or names of officials who you believe may have the records, contract number(s) if the work was completed by a contractor, date ranges or time frames, etc.). Agencies are not required to create new records, answer questions posed by requesters, or attempt to interpret a request that does not identify specific records. Additional information to assist you with clarifying your request is available on NASA's FOIA website at: https://www.nasa.gov/foia/foia-guidance/#Describing

Without the above information, your request is too broad in scope or does not specifically identify the records you seek. A request must be described with reasonably sufficient detail to enable government employees who are familiar with the subject area to locate records without placing an unreasonable burden upon the agency. 14 C.F.R. § 1206.301(b).

Once the above clarification is received, a search will be conducted for records responsive to your request. If any responsive records are located, they will be reviewed for determination of releasability. Please be assured that a FOIA specialist will respond to your request as expeditiously as possible. We appreciate your patience as we proceed with your request.

Please know that your request has been placed on hold pending receipt of the above-referenced information. Please provide the required information within 20 days of the date of this email. If we do not receive the additional information within that time frame, we will consider your request withdrawn. Please know, this is not a denial of your request, and you may submit a new request for the same information; however, you will need to provide the information as indicated above.

Sincerely,
[NASA Meatball Logo]<https://www.nasa.gov/>

NASA FOIA Unit
History and Information Services
Mary W. Jackson NASA Headquarters
300 E Street SW, Washington, DC 20546
https://www.nasa.gov/foia/

title: Mark Middleton Alpha File Folder-Title Index

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Mark Middleton Alpha File Folder-Title Index
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

An administrative folder-title index for Mark Middleton records in the Chief of Staff subgroup of Clinton Presidential Records.

Specifically, I request:

1. A list of all folder titles in the Mark Middleton series, including but not limited to OA/ID 4504, where the folder title contains the convention "[Alpha File]" or "[Alpha Files]".

2. For each matching folder title, the OA/ID number, box number, folder number if available, collection or record group, subgroup or office of origin, series or staff member, and any NARA or Clinton Library catalog identifier.

3. If a complete Middleton-series folder-title index already exists, a copy of that index or the portion covering all Mark Middleton Alpha File folders.

4. If the Library maintains an electronic folder-title list for the Middleton series, please provide that list as exported or in its available native format, such as CSV, finding-aid PDF, or another existing format, rather than re-keying folder titles manually.

5. If OA/ID 4504 is not the only Mark Middleton OA/ID in this staff series, a list of the adjacent or related Mark Middleton OA/ID numbers and their high-level descriptions.

I am requesting folder-title and administrative index information only, not the full contents of the folders at this stage.

Identifying information:

  - Known folder title variants: "[Alpha File] - Jeffrey Epstein" and "[Alpha Files] - Jeffrey Epstein [OA/ID 4504]"
  - Known OA/ID number: 4504
  - Known NARA accession identifier: #3164
  - Known collection / record group: Clinton Presidential Records
  - Known subgroup / office of origin: Chief of Staff
  - Known series / staff member: Mark Middleton
  - Known related FOIA: 2019-0820-F Segment 1
  - Known Clinton Digital Library item: https://clinton.presidentiallibraries.us/items/show/118574

Prior Library correspondence identifies a responsive folder using closely related descriptions: the April 10, 2026 response in FOIA case 2026-0425-F referred to Mark Middleton files, Box 2 (OA #4504, NARA #3164), while a later Library email transmitting the scanned folder documents referred to "[Alpha Files] - Jeffrey Epstein [OA/ID 4504]" in Box 4 of 2019-0820-F Segment 1 Mark Middleton. I am including both descriptions only to help identify the relevant Mark Middleton series and related folder-title/index records.

This request seeks the folder-title index only, so that any future substantive request can be narrowly targeted to specific Alpha File folders and avoid unnecessary review burden.

I request a waiver of all search and duplication fees under 5 U.S.C. section 552(a)(4)(A)(iii), because disclosure is in the public interest and is likely to contribute significantly to public understanding of government operations, archival processing, and Presidential records access.

If fees are expected despite this request, please notify me before incurring costs above $25.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

pest galaxy

I am unsure of what additional folder titles you are requesting from the

there is a link to the finding aid that has all of the folder titles from 
the textual collections of Mark Middleton.   The box 2 you cited looks to 
be from the original WHORM box list that was sent to us in the FRC box from 
the White House.  The box 4 is from the newly arrangmend and processed 
collection on our end.  It is the same folder title.  When a collection is 
processed, we remove the fodlers and perform perservation on the documents 
and put them in new acid free archival folders.  They are also removed from 
the original FRC box and arrangment according to the collection and 
rehoused in smaller acid free hollinger boxes.  That is why the box number 
does not match to the original WHORM inventory list created by the White 
House Office of Records Management.  You have received the list of all 
folder titles associated with Mark Middleton's textual files (including any 
Alpha Files) in the finding aid that you have linked to in your email.  

Sincerely, 

Racheal Carter-Ragan
FOIA Coordinator 
William J. Clinton Presidential Library

title: White House Office Records Regarding Digital Infrastructure Resilience and the September 2010 Facebook outage

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: White House Office Records Regarding Digital Infrastructure Resilience and the September 2010 Facebook outage
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

I hereby request access to and copies of records from the files of the National Economic Council (NEC), the Office of Science and Technology Policy (OSTP), and the Council of Economic Advisers (CEA) relating to federal assessment of private digital infrastructure vulnerabilities and any economic analysis conducted in connection with the internet service disruption of September 23–24, 2010.

For context: the September 2010 Facebook outage generated massive news coverage. The more than 2 hour outage also has a cascading impact on over 350,000 other websites that were dependent on Facebook's API. 

Some of the coverage from that day: 

https://engineering.fb.com/2010/09/23/uncategorized/more-details-on-today-s-outage/
https://medium.com/@kanishks772/the-day-facebook-died-a-cache-stampede-horror-story-that-changed-tech-forever-f7ee2dc4f627
https://www.theguardian.com/technology/2010/sep/24/facebook-server-error-solution
https://techcrunch.com/2010/09/23/facebook-downtime/

II. Scope of Search
I request that archivists search for the following categories of records, dated approximately August 1, 2010 through December 31, 2010:

Economic Impact Assessments: Internal memoranda, briefing materials, or analytical documents assessing productivity or commerce impacts of internet service disruptions, including but not limited to the September 2010 Facebook platform outage.

Infrastructure Vulnerability Policy Records: Documents discussing federal policy options regarding "single points of failure" in commercial internet infrastructure, digital economic resilience, or private-sector technology dependencies.

Correspondence: Emails, meeting minutes, or memoranda from (especially focusing on the the September 23rd/24th 2010 time period) involving NEC Director Lawrence Summers, Deputy Director Jason Furman, or Federal CTO Aneesh Chopra containing any of the following search terms: Facebook, internet outage, web disruption, technology infrastructure, digital economy, API, internet reliability, web centralization, Mark Zurckerberg. 

Interagency Communications: Any records reflecting coordination between the NEC, OSTP, CEA, Department of Commerce, or DHS regarding the economic implications of large-scale commercial internet disruptions.

III. Historical Justification
The period of 2009–2012 represents a formative era in federal digital policy, during which the Obama administration was simultaneously promoting e-government initiatives and grappling with the emerging economic centrality of private internet platforms. Records from this period documenting how senior economic advisers understood the systemic risks posed by private-sector digital infrastructure concentration would make a material contribution to the historical record of U.S. technology governance. This research is intended to support the public interest in highlighting the nation's economic dependence on one company. 

If certain parts of this request or certain portions must be redacted please provide any reasonable segregable portion of a requested record after removing or redacting those portions claimed to be exempt, explain in writing the justification for redacting the record, indicate the extent of any redactions on the portion of the record which is made available or published and where technically feasible indicate on the redacted portion itself the specific exemption(s) claimed. communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: Accurint, PSDEX, and AVCC Data Sharing Audit - Downer's Grove PD (IL)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

---
Context: Your agency appears to have an active contract with LexisNexis Risk Solutions, which includes a signed Schedule A: "Accurint Virtual Crime Center Online Subscription." That agreement includes Addendum, Section I: Cooperation with FOIA Compliance, which reads "Notwithstanding anything in the Proposal to the contrary, Vendor acknowledges that the Freedom of Information Act 5 TLCS 1401 et. seq. (*Act”) applies to the Proposal and the Act may apply to public records in possession of the Vendor. Vendor shall cooperate with the Village in its efforts to comply with the Act." title: Massachusetts Public Records Request – Prince Lobel Tye LLP Engagement

communications.subject: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: Massachusetts Public Records Request – Prince Lobel Tye LLP Engagement
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

I am requesting access to and copies of records pertaining to the MCCA’s retention of the law firm Prince Lobel Tye LLP for the workplace investigation that concluded in October 2023.

For reference, the final report produced from this engagement, titled "Report on Investigation," is dated October 13, 2023, and is currently hosted on the MCCA’s AWS server here: https://s3.amazonaws.com/massconvention/pdf/Public_Records/Report_on_Investigation_-_10.19.23.pdf 

Specifically, I am requesting the following documents:

The Contract / Engagement Letter: The fully executed engagement letter, contract, or retention agreement establishing the scope of work and financial terms between the MCCA and Prince Lobel Tye LLP for this investigation.

Conflict Waivers: Any and all conflict of interest waivers (including advance waivers for future representation) requested by Prince Lobel Tye LLP and executed by the MCCA or its representatives.

Invoices and Billing Records: All invoices, billing statements, and payment records submitted by Prince Lobel Tye LLP to the MCCA related to this engagement, from the date of retention through the present.

Please search for records after 2020 to present.

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage.

I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Brian McCarter
